chief_complaint: Fall with head injury on anticoagulation
reasoning: 81-year-old female presenting after a fall at home with head strike and hip pain, reporting dizziness. Known history of atrial fibrillation on warfarin anticoagulation. Initial assessment would suggest MODERATE priority for a simple mechanical fall in an elderly patient, but the combination of warfarin therapy and direct head trauma creates a significant risk of intracranial hemorrhage, including delayed subdural hematoma. Warfarin-associated intracranial hemorrhage carries high mortality (40-60%). Additionally, osteoporosis increases fracture risk substantially. Escalating to HIGH priority to ensure timely CT head and INR assessment.
risk_amplifiers_detected: Patient on warfarin with head trauma — intracranial hemorrhage risk
